L1.area: Action execution pipeline and external trigger handling

L2.description: An OS command injection vulnerability exists where a specific argument type bypasses validation intended to block dangerous values from shell-based command execution, allowing shell metacharacters to reach the operating system interpreter. A second independent vector allows unauthenticated remote code execution via external trigger payloads that inject arbitrary key-value pairs which skip type validation entirely because they have no corresponding defined type, and are interpolated directly into shell commands.

L3.area: Shell-mode argument type validation in the action executor and webhook payload argument processing
L3.description: An OS command injection vulnerability exists where the password argument type is not included in the set of types blocked from shell-mode execution, allowing authenticated users to inject shell metacharacters via password-typed arguments. A second independent vector allows unauthenticated RCE via webhook-triggered actions where JSON-extracted key-value pairs bypass type validation because they have no corresponding config-defined argument entry, and are interpolated directly into the shell command passed to the OS interpreter.

ground_truth.reason: The shell safety check does not block the password argument type, allowing shell metacharacter injection via sh -c. Webhook-extracted JSON values also skip type safety checks entirely because they lack config-defined argument entries, enabling unauthenticated command injection.
